text: Fatigue in ultra-endurance events is a multifactorial phenomenon that includes physiological, neuromuscular, biomechanical and cognitive factors.
Higher VO2max values are associated with improved exercise performance during ultra-endurance events.
Higher maximal aerobic speed is associated with improved ultra-endurance performance, especially during submaximal efforts sustained over a long time.
Lower oxygen cost of transport (better running economy) is associated with improved ultra-endurance performance.
Greater running experience is associated with improved performance in ultra-endurance events.
Ultra-marathon research is predominantly conducted on male athletes, with women representing less than 10% of studied participants.
